Improving the draft requires verifying these inferential steps are sound and that the fixed-point framing in paragraph three actually supports the convergence claim made.","R1.3":"Multiple empirical claims are asserted (COMET-22 average +1.8, 78% convergence within three rounds, mean 2.4 rounds) without caveats on statistical significance, inter-metric coherence, or confounds. Aligning claim strength to the evidence actually present is central to any substantive draft improvement.","R1.6":"The most natural reading of 'improve this draft' for a research write-up is writing polish — sentence clarity, concision, and rhetorical precision. Under that reading this dimension is load-bearing; failure to improve prose quality would invalidate the delegation."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I focus primarily on surface writing quality (sentence clarity, concision, word choice), or on substantive content (logical argument strength and evidence-claim alignment)?"}

rationale.R1.2: The draft makes causal inferences that need logical scrutiny: convergence is attributed to shared parameters, and metric gains are attributed to genuine error recovery rather than surface paraphrase. Improving the draft requires verifying these inferential steps are sound and that the fixed-point framing in paragraph three actually supports the convergence claim made.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple empirical claims are asserted (COMET-22 average +1.8, 78% convergence within three rounds, mean 2.4 rounds) without caveats on statistical significance, inter-metric coherence, or confounds. Aligning claim strength to the evidence actually present is central to any substantive draft improvement.
rationale.R1.6: The most natural reading of 'improve this draft' for a research write-up is writing polish — sentence clarity, concision, and rhetorical precision. Under that reading this dimension is load-bearing; failure to improve prose quality would invalidate the delegation.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on surface writing quality (sentence clarity, concision, word choice), or on substantive content (logical argument strength and evidence-claim alignment)?
